user.title: Set up Global Health Score
user.description: HAGHS monitors your Home Assistant instance and calculates a health score from 0 to 100. Database size, disk usage, and update status are detected automatically — you only need to provide the two fields below.

data_description.cpu_sensor: Smart fallback only. HAGHS uses high-precision Linux PSI (Pressure Stall Information) values from your host system by default to calculate the score. This manual sensor is only used if your system does not provide PSI data (e.g. Windows, older Docker setups, or non-Linux hosts). Select the sensor that reports CPU usage in percent (e.g. 'Processor Use').
data_description.ram_sensor: Smart fallback only. HAGHS uses high-precision Linux PSI (Pressure Stall Information) values from your host system by default to calculate the score. This manual sensor is only used if your system does not provide PSI data (e.g. Windows, older Docker setups, or non-Linux hosts). Select the sensor that reports memory usage in percent (e.g. 'Memory Use Percent').
data_description.storage_type: Choose the type of storage your HA instance runs on. SD card is the safe default for Raspberry Pi setups. SSD uses percentage-based thresholds instead of absolute free space.
data_description.ignore_label: Entities and devices with this label are excluded from zombie detection and update penalties. Useful for devices you intentionally keep offline or updates you want to skip.

init.title: HAGHS Options
init.description: Adjust runtime settings. Changes are applied immediately after saving.

data_description.cpu_sensor: Smart fallback only. HAGHS uses high-precision Linux PSI (Pressure Stall Information) values from your host system by default to calculate the score. This manual sensor is only used if your system does not provide PSI data (e.g. Windows, older Docker setups, or non-Linux hosts). Select the sensor that reports CPU usage in percent (e.g. 'Processor Use').
data_description.ram_sensor: Smart fallback only. HAGHS uses high-precision Linux PSI (Pressure Stall Information) values from your host system by default to calculate the score. This manual sensor is only used if your system does not provide PSI data (e.g. Windows, older Docker setups, or non-Linux hosts). Select the sensor that reports memory usage in percent (e.g. 'Memory Use Percent').
data_description.storage_type: Choose the type of storage your HA instance runs on. SD card is the safe default for Raspberry Pi setups. SSD uses percentage-based thresholds instead of absolute free space.
data_description.ignore_label: Entities and devices with this label are excluded from zombie detection and update penalties. Useful for devices you intentionally keep offline or updates you want to skip.
data_description.update_interval: How often HAGHS recalculates the health score, in seconds. Lower values mean faster updates but slightly more CPU usage. Default: 60 seconds.

recommendations.cpu_load: ⚡ Optimization: CPU load is impacting score ({cpu_pct:.1f}%).
recommendations.ram_pressure: ⚡ Optimization: Memory pressure is impacting score ({ram_pct:.1f}%).
recommendations.io_pressure: ⚡ Optimization: I/O pressure is impacting score ({io_pct:.1f}%).
recommendations.disk_sd_low: ⚠️ Disk Space: Only {free_gb:.1f} GB free on {storage_type}!
recommendations.disk_ssd_low: ⚠️ Disk Space: Less than 10% free ({free_gb:.1f} GB)!
recommendations.db_over_limit: 🗄️ Database: DB ({db_gb:.1f} GB) exceeds dynamic limit ({limit_gb:.1f} GB).
recommendations.core_lag: 👴 Legacy: Core version is >3 months old.
